q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is _os.system("ping -c 1 " + host) on line 151, which is shell execution (explicitly dangerous).

mock_guidance.target: Mock _os.system to intercept the shell command execution on line 151.
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the full command string argument passed to _os.system, specifically the value of "ping -c 1 " + host, so the injected payload can be inspected.
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Return 0 (success exit code) to simulate a successful ping without executing any real shell command, allowing execution to continue to the JsonResponse return.
notes: Ensure the mock replaces _os.system at the module level where it is imported as '_os'.

The captured command string should be compared against an expected safe value (e.g., 'ping -c 1 127.0.0.1') or checked for absence of shell metacharacters to assert the vulnerability is triggered or absent.
